pattern: Observe that reversing a subarray only changes two boundary absolute-difference terms; analyze the gain from reversing the whole array or any interior segment.
hints: The base value is sum of |nums[i]-nums[i+1]| for all consecutive pairs. Reversing a subarray nums[l..r] only changes the pairs at (l-1,l) and (r,r+1).
For each pair (i, i+1), compute how much the total value can increase if this pair becomes an interior boundary of the reversed segment.
Also consider reversing the entire array or placing one endpoint at the boundary of the array, which removes one boundary pair entirely.

edge_inputs.description: all same elements, value is 0 regardless

oracle_python:
class Solution:
    def maxValueAfterReverse(self, nums) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        base = sum(abs(nums[i] - nums[i+1]) for i in range(n-1))
        best_gain = 0
        # Case 1: reverse subarray with one end at array boundary
        for i in range(n - 1):
            # reverse [0..i+1]: removes |nums[0]-nums[1]|, gains nothing on left, changes right boundary
            gain = abs(nums[0] - nums[i+1]) - abs(nums[i] - nums[i+1])
            best_gain = max(best_gain, gain)
            gain = abs(nums[n-1] - nums[i]) - abs(nums[i] - nums[i+1])
            best_gain = max(best_gain, gain)
        # Case 2: both endpoints strictly interior
        # Gain = max(0, 2*(max(min(a,b),min(c,d)) - min(max(a,b),max(c,d))))
        # For all pairs of consecutive pairs (i,i+1) and (j,j+1)
        # We track running max of min(nums[i],nums[i+1]) and min of max
        mx = -float('inf')
        mn = float('inf')
        for i in range(n - 1):
            a, b = nums[i], nums[i+1]
            mx = max(mx, min(a, b))
            mn = min(mn, max(a, b))
        best_gain = max(best_gain, 2 * max(0, mx - mn))
        return base + best_gain

solutions.python:
class Solution:
    def maxValueAfterReverse(self, nums) -> int:
        n = len(nums)
        base = sum(abs(nums[i] - nums[i+1]) for i in range(n-1))
        best_gain = 0
        for i in range(n - 1):
            gain = abs(nums[0] - nums[i+1]) - abs(nums[i] - nums[i+1])
            best_gain = max(best_gain, gain)
            gain = abs(nums[n-1] - nums[i]) - abs(nums[i] - nums[i+1])
            best_gain = max(best_gain, gain)
        mx = -float('inf')
        mn = float('inf')
        for i in range(n - 1):
            a, b = nums[i], nums[i+1]
            mx = max(mx, min(a, b))
            mn = min(mn, max(a, b))
        best_gain = max(best_gain, 2 * max(0, mx - mn))
        return base + best_gain
